title: PFAS Are Stealing Bone Density From Girls. The Window to Get It Back Closes at 20.
description: New research across four cohorts finds that PFAS exposure during childhood measurably lowers bone density in adolescents — with girls hit hardest. Because humans reach peak bone mass by their early 20s and never recover lost ground, the deficit may be permanent. With 176 million Americans drinking PFAS-contaminated water and the EPA rolling back protections, the stakes are generational.
heroImageAlt: Editorial illustration for PFAS Are Stealing Bone Density From Girls. The Window to Get It Back Closes at 20.
